concept: Close adapted external material / Guest register
description: Closes the guest register opened by hasha, returning subsequent words to ordinary core Phi. The audible closer prevents an adapted name or term from silently blending into the lexicon.
sound_symbolism: It repeats the opening frame's breath and flow so the pair is heard as one device. The final rounded o supplies the closure used throughout Phi's paired frames.
grammatical_notes: Required after every hasha frame. The guest payload cannot contain standalone hasho; choose a different adaptation if that sequence would occur. External frames do not nest. The closer belongs to the frame and takes no particle of its own.

pillars.solarpunk-values: A clear ending lets communities borrow what they need without allowing temporary technical or local language to enter the shared core by accident.
pillars.peace-linguistics-practices: Audible closure keeps responsibility for an external expression distinct from the speaker's following Phi analysis.
